content:
Not sure which realm to consider pulling on as I don’t know the characters and elements very well. But I like using Caecus as the tentacles stuff is fun and he’s carrying me mostly idk how. Are there any teammates that can help him with that? Do I just target tentacle realm?

Most of my other characters are more physical sword fighting but I like far away magical mage chaos stuff more.

thread_title: Which realm on standard banner to pull for? What mechanics do elements have?

content:
So Aqueor (the tentacle realm) is pretty easy to get into, so pulling more of them would benefit Caucus more. There's not really a way to avoid "physical stuff" though, damage is damage and not set into elements but instead realms with different mechanics
you can only have 2 realm types per team however so picking ones that compliment each other if you want to mix takes some practice

Chaos gives you more keyflare for your Keeper ability, this could mean more debuffs or more ults depending on what Posse you run with

Aqueor has the tentacles which can change mode between normal, less damage but gaining shield, and more damage but you lose a tentacle at the end of the turn

Caro has a blood gauge that you can consume for healing and special embryo cards that can be consumed for a heal or saved for when a character does an ult for a bonus

Ultra has the ultra space gauge, the first card played each turn goes into it and when it's full you get a specfial free turn that uses copies of those cards as your hand

then you mix and match to your hearts content. with aqueor though I personally feel they're stronger if you mono team them because a lot of their characters give some form of tentacle buff or interact with them in some way like with free attacks or getting buffs for being in certain modes
thread_title: Which realm on standard banner to pull for? What mechanics do elements have?

content: A note that when playing ultra, you can click the left hand side of the gauge and destroy one card in ultra space (delaying your extra free turn) to add an insight (+1 draw +1 arithmetica) card to your hand!

thread_title: How gacha system work? How get skin?

content: There are reruns, and the upcoming 2nd Anniversary might have a rotating banner where every day it swaps to a different limited unit. (Or so I'm told that's how it was during the 1st Anniversary.)
thread_title: How gacha system work? How get skin?

content: As for getting Pollux, you should be able to get enough by just playing the game as a new player to get him before his banner goes away. Though if I'm going to be honest, if you care about the meta, I'd suggest going for Castor and Castor's wheel first.
thread_title: How gacha system work? How get skin?

content: Pollux banner is up. You can pull for either characters or their wheel. The gacha have a pity after about 30 pulls, you're guaranteed an SSR (Not the rate-up). If you still haven't got the rate up after 2 pity (60 pulls), you're guaranteed to get them after your next pity which is the 90th pull. There's no information on Doresain rerun as of now since he's relatively new. For his skin, you can buy using the premium currency (silver prime).
thread_title: How gacha system work? How get skin?

content:
What do you mean by wheel?

And the silver prime you can only receive if you spend money, no other way?

Yeah I also like Castor but I like Pollux more and want to try get him first
thread_title: How gacha system work? How get skin?

content:
Wheel is just the term for main equipment your character have. Each character have their own wheel with passives that complements their playstyle.
No, there's no other way to obtain silver prime other than using real money.
You can pull for Pollux, it's fine. If you're early in the story, you can farm a lot of silvers to buy cores. People are generally skeptical of new characters since they're still figuring them out so don't be afraid to pull what you want.
